# `phenotype-router` — LLM-facing context

> Mirror the conventions in `pheno-port-adapter/llms.txt` and
> `pheno-tracing/llms.txt`. This file is the canonical context for
> LLM-based code assistants and code-review tools.

## What this crate does

`phenotype-router` is the **decision layer** of the Phenotype router
architecture. It receives a `Request` and returns a `Response` whose
`Decision` is one of `Allow | Defer | Deny(reason)`. It is a
hexagonal L4 Port/Adapter primitive (ADR-038) with OTel-native OTLP
span emission (ADR-012 / ADR-036B).

## When to use this crate

- You are building a new LLM-routing service and need a substrate-grade
  decision layer with built-in OTLP span emission.
- You are adding a new adapter (Bifrost replacement, SmartFallback,
  plugin pre-routing) and want to plug into a fleet-wide contract.
- You are wiring observability for routing decisions and want one
  canonical span shape across the fleet (`phenotype.router.decision`,
  per ADR-052 §3).

## When NOT to use this crate

- You need the **transport layer** (use `phenotype-gateway`).
- You need the **plugin chain runtime** (v0.5.0; v0.3.0 ships the
  decision-layer contract that plugins will plug into).
- You need a **full OTel SDK** with batching, resource attributes, etc.
  — the recorder is a thin span producer; consumers wire the SDK.

## Public surface

```rust
// Decision port contract (ADR-050 / ADR-038)
pub trait DecisionLayer: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn adapter_kind(&self) -> &str { self.name() }
    fn health(&self) -> Result<(), DecisionError> { Ok(()) }
    fn decide(&self, req: &Request) -> Response;
}

// In-tree adapters
pub struct BifrostAdapter;     // stub mirroring Bifrost's allow/deny rules
pub struct HelloWorld;         // no-op fixture, returns Allow

// OTel substrate (ADR-012 / ADR-036B / ADR-052 §3)
pub struct OtlpDecisionRecorder { /* ... */ }
pub struct TraceOperation { /* name, kind, attributes */ }
pub trait TracePort: Send + Sync { fn submit(&self, op: TraceOperation) -> TraceResult; }
pub struct InMemoryTracePort;   // for tests

// SDK contracts (ADR-052 §1)
#[async_trait] pub trait LlmPort: Send + Sync { /* ... */ }
pub trait DecisionPlugin: Send + Sync { /* ... */ }
#[async_trait] pub trait ConnectorPort: Send + Sync { /* ... */ }

// Chaos matrix (ADR-052 §4)
pub struct ChaosMatrix { /* 5 fault kinds */ }
pub enum ChaosKind { Timeout, Malformed, Unreachable, Transient, Overload }
pub trait ChaosInjector { fn inject(&self, s: &ChaosScenario) -> FaultOutcome; }
```

## Code patterns

### 5-line minimal use

```rust
use phenotype_router::{BifrostAdapter, DecisionLayer, Request};
let adapter = BifrostAdapter::new();
let req = Request::new("user:42", "weather");
let resp = adapter.decide(&req);
assert_eq!(resp.decision.kind_str(), "allow");
```

### Record a span (in-tree in-memory port)

```rust
use phenotype_router::otel::{OtelConfig, OtlpDecisionRecorder};
use std::sync::Arc;

let recorder = OtlpDecisionRecorder::in_memory(OtelConfig::default());
let adapter = BifrostAdapter::new();
let req = Request::new("user:42", "weather");
let resp = adapter.decide(&req);
recorder.record(&adapter, &req, &resp); // submits to InMemoryTracePort
```

### Custom TracePort (consumer-wired OTel SDK)

```rust
use phenotype_router::otel::{TracePort, TraceOperation, TraceResult, TraceStatus, OtlpDecisionRecorder};
use std::sync::Arc;

struct MyOtelPort;
impl TracePort for MyOtelPort {
    fn submit(&self, op: TraceOperation) -> TraceResult {
        // forward `op` to opentelemetry-otlp / jaeger / etc.
        TraceResult { trace_id: op.trace_id, span_id: op.span_id, status: TraceStatus::Ok }
    }
}

let recorder = OtlpDecisionRecorder::with_port(
    Arc::new(MyOtelPort),
    OtelConfig { service_name: "my-svc".into(), ..Default::default() },
);
```

## Quality bar (must keep green)

- **Tests:** 56 total (32 unit + 6 chaos + 6 hello + 4 otlp + 8 e2e).
- **Coverage:** 60 % line (federated-service tier, ADR-040).
- **CI:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (build + test + chaos + bench + clippy).
- **Docs:** `SPEC.md` (1 page) + `README.md` + `docs/concept.md` + `docs/architecture.md`.
- **Observability:** `OtlpDecisionRecorder` (ADR-012 / ADR-036B / ADR-052 §3).
- **Span name:** `phenotype.router.decision` (PINNED; do not change).

## Related ADRs

- ADR-023 (substrate placement) — `docs/adr/2026-06-15/ADR-023-agent-effort-governance.md`.
- ADR-038 (hexagonal L4 Port) — `docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-038-hexagonal-port-adapter-l4-policy.md`.
- ADR-040 (test coverage gates per tier) — `docs/adr/2026-06-18/ADR-040-test-coverage-gates-per-tier.md`.
- ADR-050 (router rebuild) — `docs/adr/2026-06-20/ADR-050-router-rebuild.md`.
- ADR-051 (Bifrost-as-library) — `docs/adr/2026-06-20/ADR-051-bifrost-as-library.md`.
- ADR-052 (plugin SDK spec) — `docs/adr/2026-06-20/ADR-052-plugin-sdk-spec.md`.
- ADR-012 / ADR-036B (pheno-tracing substrate canonical).

## Related substrate crates

- `pheno-port-adapter` — hexagonal L4 reference impl.
- `pheno-tracing` — tracing substrate (v15 follow-up).
- `pheno-otel` — OTLP wire-format exporter.
- `pheno-config` — config substrate.
- `pheno-worklog-schema` — worklog v2.1 parser.
